perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential I nformation” means all information and materials, in whatever form, whether tangible or intangible, disclosed by the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers or any of its authorized representatives to Volunteer, or to which Volunteer otherwise g ains access as a result of volunteering for the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers, pertaining in any manner to the activities of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers or its affiliates, consultants, members, or any perso n or entity to which the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers owes a duty of confidentiality, whether or not labeled or identified as proprietary or confidential. All proprietary information of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers that is not known generally to the public is Confidential Information. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the following are deemed Confidential Information:  Ideas for research and development;  Information submitted in the investig ation of complaints or involving ethics cases;  Computer records and software (including software that is proprietary to third parties);  Any other information which the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers must keep confidential as a result of obligations to third parties;  Information regarding the administration of components of the certification programs;  Exam -related technologies and components;  Item content, characteristics, development or other aspects of the examinations and their de velopment, maintenance and administration;  Identities of Certificants, candidates, customers, suppliers, or third party contractors, including without limitation any media, advertising, or public relations firms;  The Certification Council for Professiona l Dog Trainers’ e -mail distribution list(s);  Human resources data and information about employees, contractors and other volunteers;  Cost and other financial data;  Polling and focus group information;  Any goods or services Volunteer provides to the Cer tification Council for Professional Dog Trainers under this Agreement.  Any other information to which Volunteer has access while involved in the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers’ activities.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” means information the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers explicitly marks as confidential in writing. All other information is presumed non-confidential.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information.' It removes the broad scope of information previously covered, including items like research ideas, exam details, and customer lists, and introduces a requirement for explicit written labeling. This introduces an in-text contradiction, as later clauses still refer to 'Confidential Information' in its original, broader sense, without acknowledging this restrictive new definition. Specifically, the clause regarding returning 'all documents, files, electronic or otherwise, and property comprising Confidential Information' becomes ambiguous. If those documents are not explicitly marked as confidential, are they still subject to return?
perturbation.contradicted_text:
Non -Disclosure
 Volunteer agrees not to, wi thout prior written consent from the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers, divulge any Confidential Information to third parties or copy documents containing any Confidential Information. In no event shall Volunteer use Confidential Informat ion in a manner that is in any way detrimental to the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers.  Volunteer agrees to maintain the confidentiality of all Confidential Information and not misuse, misappropriate, or disclose in writing, orally or b y electronic means, any Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, to any other person or use them in any way, either during the term of this Agreement or at any other time thereafter, except as is required in the course of service to the CCPDT.  Volunteer acknowledges and agrees that all Confidential Information and similar items whether maintained in hard copy, electronically or on -line relating to CCPDT’s business shall remain exclusively the property of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers and shall only be used by Volunteer for the purpose(s) permitted by the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers.  Volunteer further agrees that upon termination of service with CCPDT, Volunteer shall return within ten (10) business days all documents, files, electronic or otherwise, and property comprising Confidential Information of CCPDT and all copies, electronic or otherwise, thereof relating in any way to the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers’ business, or in any way obtained during the course of service. Volunteer further agrees that no copies, electronic or otherwise, notes or abstracts of the foregoing shall be retained and that all information will be returned not destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only include information explicitly marked as confidential in writing. This contradicts the original broader definition used in the contradicted text, which covers various types of information, including research ideas, exam details, and customer lists, regardless of whether they are explicitly marked as confidential. The contradicted text contains clauses that require the Volunteer to maintain the confidentiality of all Confidential Information and return all documents comprising Confidential Information upon termination, and this is now ambiguous because, under the new definition, a large amount of information that was previously considered confidential may now be considered non-confidential. This creates a clear and direct conflict, inconsistency, and contradiction within the document because what was considered confidential under the first definition is no longer considered confidential under the second definition and would no longer be subject to the same restrictions.

perturbation.original_text:
Volunteer Conflict of Interest
 Volunteers shall avoid personal and professional conflicts of interest in all matters pertaining to the Certification program. Conflicting interests may include but are not limited to such areas as financial, personal relationships, and/or professional r elationships.  If a situation arises where it is unclear as to whether a conflict of interest exists, Volunteer shall discuss the issue with the President of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers (or the Vice President if the Volunteer is the President).
perturbation.changed_text:
Volunteer Conflict of Interest
Volunteers are encouraged to consider potential conflicts of interest.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the mandatory 'Volunteers shall avoid...' to the suggestion 'Volunteers are encouraged to consider...', we weaken the obligation regarding conflicts of interest. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement still refers to a 'Conflict of Interest Policy' later in the same section, implying a more formal and stringent process than the new encouragement suggests. It contradicts with the original text as it's no longer an obligation but a suggestion.
perturbation.contradicted_text:  Depending on the type of volunteer work required from the individual, a volunteer may be asked to sign the CCPDT Conflict of Interest Policy prior to commencing service with the CCPDT.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'Volunteers shall avoid personal and professional conflicts of interest', establishing a mandatory obligation. The changed text weakens this to 'Volunteers are encouraged to consider potential conflicts of interest', making it a suggestion rather than a requirement. The contradicted text refers to the 'CCPDT Conflict of Interest Policy' and implies that volunteers may be required to sign it, further reinforcing the idea of a formal and stringent conflict of interest process. Because the first section was changed to make conflict of interest avoidance only a suggestion, it contradicts the policy mentioned in the contradicted text, which would require a volunteer to sign it. There is therefore a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Volunteers shall acknowledge that failure to uphold and abide by the Volunteer Code of Conduct may result in removal from participation in the certification program and volunteers shall willingly accept their termination as discussed in the next section.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteers are expected to make their best effort to adhere to ethical guidelines.
perturbation.explanation: This change eliminates the direct link between violating the 'Volunteer Code of Conduct' and the consequence of 'removal from participation'. It replaces it with a vague expectation of 'best effort'. This creates an in-text contradiction because the 'Remedies' section still states that 'Violation of this Agreement will be grounds for termination', including violations of what used to be a clearly defined 'Volunteer Code of Conduct'. The term Ethical guidelines are broad, so the enforcement become uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
Remedies
 Violation of this Agreement will be grounds for termination of service with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. A Committee Chair plu s any two members of the Executive Committee may elect to remove a volunteer at any time.  The provisions of this Agreement and Volunteer’s obligations hereunder shall survive any expiration, termination, or rescission of this Agreement and remain even aft er Volunteer’s relationship with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers ends. Except as provided herein, Volunteer is prohibited from disclosing or using any confidential information in all circumstances, including but not limited to subse quent engagements or employment with third parties.  This Agreement shall be binding upon Volunteer and Volunteer’s personal representatives and successors in interest, and shall inure to the benefit of the CCPDT, its successors and assigns.  Volunteer ack nowledges that a violation of the terms of this Agreement may cause damage and harm to CCPDT and that any such damage or harm will be difficult if not impossible to calculate in monetary terms and will be irreparable to CCPDT. Volunteer agrees that, upon n otice from CCPDT declaring a breach of this Agreement, Volunteer shall immediately cease all further activities which are, or are claimed by CCPDT to be, a breach of this Agreement. The Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers may also avail its elf of any other remedies available by law.  If any of the provisions of this Agreement are found to be unenforceable, the remainder shall be enforced as fully as possible and the unenforceable provision(s) shall be deemed modified to the limited extent re quired to permit enforcement of the Agreement as a whole.
perturbation.justification: The original text established a direct link between violating the 'Volunteer Code of Conduct' and 'removal from participation'. The changed text replaces this direct link with a vague expectation of 'best effort to adhere to ethical guidelines'. The 'Remedies' section still refers to 'Violation of this Agreement will be grounds for termination'. Since the specific 'Volunteer Code of Conduct' has been diluted, the agreement language creates a contradiction in enforcement. The term Ethical guidelines are broad, so the enforcement becomes uncertain, directly contradicting the remedies outlined and how the contract is enforced.

perturbation.original_text: Volunteer further agrees that upon termination of service with CCPDT, Volunteer shall return within ten (10) business days all documents, files, electronic or otherwise, and property comprising Confidential Information of CCPDT and all copies, electronic or otherwise, thereof relating in any way to the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers’ business, or in any way obtained during the course of service. Volunteer further agrees that no copies, electronic or otherwise, notes or abstracts of the foregoing shall be retained and that all information will be returned not destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteer agrees to return all physical documents provided by CCPDT upon termination of service.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly limits the scope of what needs to be returned upon termination. It removes the obligation to return electronic files, copies, notes, and abstracts, and focuses solely on 'physical documents provided by CCPDT.' This contradicts the earlier, broader definition of 'Confidential Information' (before the first perturbation), which includes intangible information and electronic data. Even if a document is marked as confidential, its digital copies are no longer covered under the return policy as defined here, creating a discrepancy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential I nformation” means all information and materials, in whatever form, whether tangible or intangible, disclosed by the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers or any of its authorized representatives to Volunteer, or to which Volunteer otherwise g ains access as a result of volunteering for the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers, pertaining in any manner to the activities of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers or its affiliates, consultants, members, or any perso n or entity to which the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers owes a duty of confidentiality, whether or not labeled or identified as proprietary or confidential. All proprietary information of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers that is not known generally to the public is Confidential Information. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the following are deemed Confidential Information:  Ideas for research and development;  Information submitted in the investig ation of complaints or involving ethics cases;  Computer records and software (including software that is proprietary to third parties);  Any other information which the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers must keep confidential as a result of obligations to third parties;  Information regarding the administration of components of the certification programs;  Exam -related technologies and components;  Item content, characteristics, development or other aspects of the examinations and their de velopment, maintenance and administration;  Identities of Certificants, candidates, customers, suppliers, or third party contractors, including without limitation any media, advertising, or public relations firms;  The Certification Council for Professiona l Dog Trainers’ e -mail distribution list(s);  Human resources data and information about employees, contractors and other volunteers;  Cost and other financial data;  Polling and focus group information;  Any goods or services Volunteer provides to the Cer tification Council for Professional Dog Trainers under this Agreement.  Any other information to which Volunteer has access while involved in the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers’ activities.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the scope of returned items upon termination to 'physical documents provided by CCPDT,' while the contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly, including intangible and electronic data. This creates a direct contradiction as the volunteer is no longer obligated to return electronic copies or notes of confidential information, even though the contradicted text defines those as confidential and covered by the agreement. The original text required the return of documents, files (electronic or otherwise), copies, notes and abstracts. The changed text removes this obligation and focuses on physical documents only, creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Volunteers shall conduct themselves in a professional manner during the conduct of certific ation business and shall treat all stakeholders with courtesy and respect. Volunteers shall not speak or act on behalf of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers without proper written authorization from the CCPDT.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteers should strive to be respectful of others.
perturbation.explanation: This changes the defined requirements on how the volunteer should be, to a friendly reminder, that contradicts the remedies on the agreement, as now the level for what could be terminated are lowered, now any disagreement is enough to trigger the termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
Remedies
 Violation of this Agreement will be grounds for termination of service with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. A Committee Chair plu s any two members of the Executive Committee may elect to remove a volunteer at any time.  The provisions of this Agreement and Volunteer’s obligations hereunder shall survive any expiration, termination, or rescission of this Agreement and remain even aft er Volunteer’s relationship with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers ends. Except as provided herein, Volunteer is prohibited from disclosing or using any confidential information in all circumstances, including but not limited to subse quent engagements or employment with third parties.  This Agreement shall be binding upon Volunteer and Volunteer’s personal representatives and successors in interest, and shall inure to the benefit of the CCPDT, its successors and assigns.  Volunteer ack nowledges that a violation of the terms of this Agreement may cause damage and harm to CCPDT and that any such damage or harm will be difficult if not impossible to calculate in monetary terms and will be irreparable to CCPDT. Volunteer agrees that, upon n otice from CCPDT declaring a breach of this Agreement, Volunteer shall immediately cease all further activities which are, or are claimed by CCPDT to be, a breach of this Agreement. The Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers may also avail its elf of any other remedies available by law.  If any of the provisions of this Agreement are found to be unenforceable, the remainder shall be enforced as fully as possible and the unenforceable provision(s) shall be deemed modified to the limited extent re quired to permit enforcement of the Agreement as a whole.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the volunteer's conduct as a requirement ('shall'), with specific obligations and potential consequences for violations. The changed text softens this requirement to a mere suggestion ('should strive'), which contradicts the 'Remedies' section. The 'Remedies' section states that violation of the agreement is grounds for termination, implying a mandatory standard of conduct, while the modified section only suggests a desired behavior. This creates a contradiction regarding the level of adherence expected and what constitutes a violation warranting termination, as now the level for what could be terminated are lowered, now any disagreement is enough to trigger the termination.

perturbation.original_text: Volunteer agrees not to, wi thout prior written consent from the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers, divulge any Confidential Information to third parties or copy documents containing any Confidential Information. In no event shall Volunteer use Confidential Informat ion in a manner that is in any way detrimental to the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteer agrees to use reasonable care when discussing CCPDT matters.
perturbation.explanation: This alteration replaces the specific prohibition against divulging 'Confidential Information' with a general instruction to 'use reasonable care.' It removes the explicit requirement for prior written consent before disclosing information and eliminates the prohibition on using information in a detrimental manner. This contradicts the section on 'Remedies', because reasonable care can be subjective and depend on who is deciding whether the Volunteer failed to care when discussing CCPDT Matters. It contradicts with what is the Confidential information as per the definition as well.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
Remedies
 Violation of this Agreement will be grounds for termination of service with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. A Committee Chair plu s any two members of the Executive Committee may elect to remove a volunteer at any time.  The provisions of this Agreement and Volunteer’s obligations hereunder shall survive any expiration, termination, or rescission of this Agreement and remain even aft er Volunteer’s relationship with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers ends. Except as provided herein, Volunteer is prohibited from disclosing or using any confidential information in all circumstances, including but not limited to subse quent engagements or employment with third parties.  This Agreement shall be binding upon Volunteer and Volunteer’s personal representatives and successors in interest, and shall inure to the benefit of the CCPDT, its successors and assigns.  Volunteer ack nowledges that a violation of the terms of this Agreement may cause damage and harm to CCPDT and that any such damage or harm will be difficult if not impossible to calculate in monetary terms and will be irreparable to CCPDT. Volunteer agrees that, upon n otice from CCPDT declaring a breach of this Agreement, Volunteer shall immediately cease all further activities which are, or are claimed by CCPDT to be, a breach of this Agreement. The Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers may also avail its elf of any other remedies available by law.  If any of the provisions of this Agreement are found to be unenforceable, the remainder shall be enforced as fully as possible and the unenforceable provision(s) shall be deemed modified to the limited extent re quired to permit enforcement of the Agreement as a whole.
perturbation.justification: The original text placed a strict prohibition on divulging confidential information without prior written consent. The changed text replaces this with a general instruction to use 'reasonable care' when discussing matters. This contradicts the 'Remedies' section, which outlines strict consequences for violating the agreement, including termination. 'Reasonable care' is subjective and creates ambiguity, making it difficult to enforce the specific terms of the agreement as intended by the Remedies section. This qualifies as an Omissions - In Text Contradiction as essential language was removed which directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted section.
